title: The Rumor Mill
desc: Information is currency. Today you're both buyer and seller.

steps.text:
Every district in UnderWorld City has a gathering spot where creatures trade gossip like currency. Today's spot is a tavern near the Colosseum, packed with fighters, handlers, and those who make their living knowing things.

You sit at the bar. Within minutes, a creature slides onto the stool next to you and starts talking without preamble.

"Heard the garrison at the southern tunnels is getting doubled. Could mean expansion. Could mean they found something. Could mean the commander has enemies and wants more bodies between him and them."

The creature looks at you expectantly. The exchange rate is clear: a rumor for a rumor.

options.label: Trade — share what you saw at the market
Listen but don't trade — just observe

steps.text:
You mention the garrison movements you noticed near the Colosseum — more patrols, tighter schedules. The creature nods, filing it away.

"Increased patrols plus doubled garrison. That's not expansion. That's defensive." They lower their voice. "Something spooked them. Don't know what. But when the UnderWorld gets defensive, it means they're not sure what they're defending against."

Other creatures drift in and out of the conversation, each adding a piece. A supply route was rerouted. A tunnel was sealed — not collapsed, sealed deliberately. A patrol came back from the deep tunnels quiet and wouldn't talk about what they saw.

None of it adds up. Each piece is a fragment without a picture. But the fragments keep coming, and the shape they suggest is unsettling.

You leave with more questions than answers. In the UnderWorld, that's normal. What's not normal is how many people are asking the same questions.

options.label: Head home and think about what you heard

steps.text:
You listen. The tavern talks around you — a stream of half-truths, exaggerations, and occasional facts buried in noise.

A fighter complains about training schedules being moved. A handler talks about new battlegear requisitions — more defensive pieces, fewer weapons. A creature who works in the archives mentions a file being pulled — something old, from the founding records.

None of them are talking to each other. None of them see the pattern. But from your stool, listening to all of them at once, you do. Something is changing in the UnderWorld. Not on the surface — underneath. In the logistics. In the schedules. In the quiet decisions that nobody announces.

The bartender catches you listening and gives you a look that says: you hear it too.

You nod. The bartender goes back to pouring drinks. Some things don't need words.

options.label: Leave the tavern knowing more than you should
